title: CryptoCbGuard/Client drop order is unsound use-after-free
description: CryptoCbGuard stores a raw pointer into Client's pinned whClientContext but has no lifetime tie to Client. If Client is dropped first (Rust allows this — the caller owns both), wolfCrypt's global table holds a dangling pointer; any subsequent wolfCrypt call using DEV_ID is a use-after-free. The comment in cryptocb.rs says 'caller is responsible for this ordering' but there is zero enforcement. This will generate hard-to-diagnose corruption reports from users who wrote the idiomatic but incorrect drop order.
